description: Post-deployment validation data for all task flows. Contains manual steps, phases, and detailed checklist items. ⚠️ AGENTS: Do NOT read this file directly — use validate-items.py instead.

ai_governance_checklist: Data Agent bound to appropriate data source (Lakehouse, Warehouse, KQL Database, or Semantic Model)
Data Agent greeting and instructions configured
Natural language query returns correct data (test 3-5 representative questions)
Ontology created (if using) with business terms mapped to data fields
variable_library_checklist: Variable Library item exists in workspace
Active value set matches current deployment stage
Consuming items (Notebooks, Pipelines, Shortcuts) reference the Variable Library

app-backend.description: Post-deployment validation for App Backend task flow.

manual_steps.action: Create tables/schema, configure security (RLS/OLS if needed), verify OneLake mirroring
Create containers, configure partition keys, load sample data, verify OneLake mirroring
Select data sources, choose tables/views to expose, define relationships, test queries
Write function code, add PyPI libraries, publish functions, test with sample inputs
Add variables, create value sets per stage, set active value set, configure item references
Configure connection to database (via OneLake mirror), set query mode (Direct Lake / DirectQuery)
Connect to Semantic Model, build visualizations, configure refresh
Add data sources (up to 5), select tables, write instructions, add example queries
Define entity types/properties/relationships, bind to data sources, refresh graph

Foundation: SQL Database OR Cosmos DB exists in workspace
Tables/containers are created with correct schema
Sample data is loaded for testing
OneLake mirroring is active (data visible in Delta format)
SQL analytics endpoint is available (for SQL Database)
Cross-database queries work from SQL analytics endpoint
Configuration: Variable Library item exists in workspace
Variables are defined with correct types and default values
Value sets exist for each deployment stage (Dev/PPE/Prod)
Active value set is correct for current workspace
Item reference variables point to correct workspace + item IDs
Consuming items (Notebooks, Pipelines, Shortcuts) reference the Variable Library

GraphQL API: GraphQL API item exists in workspace
Data source(s) connected (SQL Database, Cosmos DB, or Lakehouse SQL endpoint)
Schema auto-generated with expected types and fields
Relationships defined between related entities (if applicable)
Test query returns expected results in built-in editor
Mutations work for write operations (via stored procedures if SQL Database)
Endpoint URL is accessible and returns valid responses
Permissions are configured (who can query the API)
User Data Functions: User Data Functions item exists in workspace
Required PyPI libraries are installed (pandas, requests, etc.)
Functions are published (no pending changes indicator)
Each function tested with sample inputs in Run Only mode
REST endpoints are accessible from external HTTP clients
Functions that write to database successfully persist data
Error handling returns meaningful error messages

Analytics: Semantic Model exists and is connected to database (via OneLake mirror)
Query mode is configured (Direct Lake for optimal performance)
Data refresh succeeds (or Direct Lake auto-refreshes)
Semantic Model is accessible to report consumers

Report: Report exists and is connected to Semantic Model
Filters and slicers work as expected
Report is shared with intended audience
Data Agent: Data Agent item exists in workspace
Example queries added for few-shot learning (if applicable)
Test questions return accurate, data-backed answers
Ontology: Entity types defined with properties and relationships
Data bindings configured to OneLake sources

basic-data-analytics.description: Post-deployment validation for Basic Data Analytics task flow.

manual_steps.action: Publish dataflow; set up refresh credentials
Bind to Warehouse (if Direct Lake); set refresh credentials

Ingestion: Ingestion item created (Copy Job / Dataflow / Pipeline)

basic-machine-learning-models.description: Post-deployment validation for Basic Machine Learning Models task flow.

manual_steps.action: Verify permissions; configure for ML data
Configure Spark settings; install ML libraries; publish
Set default lakehouse; verify environment attachment
Run experiment first (at least 1 run required), then register best model. Cannot validate until experiment has completed runs.
Verify semantic model binding; check predictions data

Data Exploration: Notebook can read data from Lakehouse

conversational-analytics.description: Post-deployment validation for Conversational Analytics task flow.

manual_steps.action: Verify data is populated from upstream task flow
Verify data is populated from upstream task flow
Bind to storage; validate measures and relationships
Create and bind to Semantic Model; configure access

Foundation: Storage item exists and contains data (Lakehouse or Warehouse)
Data is current (ingestion pipeline from upstream task flow is running)
Workspace permissions configured for agent consumers
Semantic Layer: Semantic Model created and bound to storage
Direct Lake mode configured (if using Lakehouse)
Data refresh working (if using Import mode)
Agent Deployment: Access permissions set (who can chat with the agent)
Agent published and accessible to end users
Agent Testing: Natural language query returns correct data (test 3-5 representative questions)
Agent correctly handles ambiguous questions (asks for clarification)
Agent respects data security (row-level security if applicable)
Agent handles 'I don't know' gracefully (questions outside data scope)
Response latency is acceptable for interactive use
Optional Governance: Ontology created (if using) with business terms mapped to semantic model fields
Activator alerts configured (if monitoring agent usage patterns)

data-analytics-sql-endpoint.description: Post-deployment validation for Data Analytics Using SQL Endpoint task flow.

manual_steps.action: Verify permissions; SQL analytics endpoint auto-created
Set default lakehouse; verify environment attachment
Bind to Lakehouse; configure Direct Lake (recommended) or Import/DirectQuery

options: OPTION B: Spark Job Definition (for scheduled)

event-analytics.description: Post-deployment validation for Event Analytics task flow.

manual_steps.action: Create KQL databases; configure retention policies
Configure source connections (Event Hubs, Kafka, etc.)
Bind to KQL Database (within Eventhouse); configure auto-refresh

Real-Time Ingestion: Eventstream created and connected to source
Visualization: Report created with correct data bindings

event-medallion.description: Post-deployment validation for Event Medallion task flow.

manual_steps.action: Configure tiles; bind to KQL Database; set refresh

Visualization: Real-Time Dashboard tiles connected to Gold

general.description: Post-deployment validation for General task flow.

manual_steps.action: Bind to data source; configure Direct Lake (recommended) or Import/DirectQuery

lambda.description: Post-deployment validation for Lambda task flow.

manual_steps.action: Bind to Warehouse; configure Direct Lake (recommended) or Import/DirectQuery

Serving Layer: Semantic Model bound to batch data

medallion.description: Post-deployment validation for Medallion task flow.

manual_steps.action: Set default lakehouse; verify environment attachment
Bind to Gold layer (Lakehouse or Warehouse); configure Direct Lake (recommended) or Import/DirectQuery

options: OPTION A: Lakehouse Gold (for Spark, ML, Delta Lake, read-only via SQL)
OPTION B: Warehouse Gold (for T-SQL read/write, BI, stored procedures)

semantic-governance.description: Post-deployment validation for Semantic Governance task flow.

manual_steps.action: Verify data is populated from upstream task flow
Bind to storage; validate measures and relationships
Define entity types and edge types
Create in Fabric workspace portal; author graph queries (portal-only)
(Optional) Create and bind to Semantic Model + Ontology

Foundation: Lakehouse (or Warehouse) exists and contains data
Workspace permissions configured for governance team
Data sources identified for ontology scope
Semantic Model: Semantic Model created and bound to storage
Field descriptions populated (these inform the Ontology)
Model endorsed (Certified or Promoted) if using endorsement
Ontology Definition: Business domains defined (e.g., Sales, Finance, Operations)
Key business terms created with definitions
Terms mapped to Semantic Model fields/tables
Knowledge Graph: Graph Model created with entity types and relationships
Entity types correspond to ontology business terms
Graph Queryset created with representative queries
Graph queries return expected traversal results
Graph relationships are consistent with Semantic Model relationships
Governance Validation: Business terms are consistent across Ontology and Semantic Model
No orphaned terms (terms without data mapping)
No unmapped fields (important data fields without business terms)
Data Agent configured with ontology context (if using)
External Integration: Purview catalog synced (if using Microsoft Purview)
Lineage tracked from source through governance layer

sensitive-data-insights.description: Post-deployment validation for Sensitive Data Insights task flow.
ai_governance_checklist_override: Data Agent bound to anonymized Warehouse or Semantic Model (with RLS/OLS)
Data Agent greeting and instructions configured
Data Agent access permissions set (⚠️ verify RLS/OLS inheritance)
Natural language query returns correct data (test 3-5 representative questions)
Agent does NOT expose masked/redacted data
Ontology created (if using) with business terms mapped to data fields

manual_steps.action: Configure strict permissions; enable audit logging

Foundation (Secured): Raw Lakehouse created with strict permissions
Masked Lakehouse created with restricted access
Audit logging enabled on all storage

sub_items: Managed identity used (no secrets in code)

Secure Visualization: Reports render correctly per user role
Security Validation: No PII visible in aggregated layer

translytical.description: Post-deployment validation for Translytical task flow.

manual_steps.action: Verify permissions; configure schema for writeback
Bind to SQL Database; configure Direct Lake (via OneLake mirroring) or DirectQuery (for live OLTP data)
Enable writeback features; configure data entry

Foundation (Transactional Storage): Write permissions granted to app identity
Writeback Validation: Users can enter data in reports
Entered data persists to SQL Database
